file.content:
name: "Setup Node & Bun"
description: "Setup to run task in functions"
runs:
  using: "composite"
  steps:
    - uses: oven-sh/setup-bun@v2
      with:
        bun-version: 1.2.18

    # - uses: useblacksmith/cache@v5
    - uses: actions/cache@v3
      name: Setup bun cache
      with:
        path: ~/.bun/install/cache
        key: ${{ runner.os }}-bun-store-v4-${{ hashFiles('**/bun.lockb', '**/bun.lock') }}
        restore-keys: |
          ${{ runner.os }}-bun-store-v4

    - name: Set TODAY env variable
      run: echo "TODAY=$(date +'%Y-%m-%d')" >> $GITHUB_ENV
      shell: bash

    - name: Install dependencies
      run: bun install --frozen-lockfile
      shell: bash

    # - name: Clean Modules
    #   run: bun deps:clean
    #   shell: bash

file.content:
name: CI

on:
  pull_request:
  push:
    branches:
      - main

jobs:
  verify:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Setup Bun
        uses: oven-sh/setup-bun@v2
        with:
          bun-version: 1.3.5

      - name: Cache bun install
        uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: ~/.bun/install/cache
          key: ${{ runner.os }}-bun-${{ hashFiles('**/bun.lock') }}
          restore-keys: |
            ${{ runner.os }}-bun-

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: bun install --frozen-lockfile

      - name: Verify lockfile unchanged
        run: git diff --exit-code bun.lock

      - name: Verify
        run: bunx turbo run lint check-types test build
        env:
          PG_CONNECTION_STRING: ${{ secrets.PG_CONNECTION_STRING }}
          CLICKHOUSE_URL: ${{ secrets.CLICKHOUSE_URL }}
          CLICKHOUSE_USERNAME: ${{ secrets.CLICKHOUSE_USERNAME }}
          CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD: ${{ secrets.CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD }}
          CLICKHOUSE_DB: ${{ secrets.CLICKHOUSE_DB }}

  deploy:
    if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' && github.event_name == 'push'
    needs: verify
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Setup Fly.io
        uses: superfly/flyctl-actions/setup-flyctl@master

      - name: Deploy
        run: flyctl deploy --remote-only
        env:
          FLY_API_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.FLY_API_TOKEN }}

content.thinking:
Now I understand the setup. The flick project has:
1. A composite action at `.github/actions/setup/action.yml` that sets up Bun, caches dependencies, and installs them
2. Uses `useblacksmith/cache@v5` (commented out, currently using `actions/cache@v3`) for caching `~/.bun/install/cache`
3. The CI workflow references this action

The rudel project currently:
1. Has no composite action - setup is inline in the CI workflow
2. Uses `actions/cache@v4` for bun cache
3. Runs on `ubuntu-latest` (not blacksmith)


4. The user wants to switch to blacksmith runners with blacksmith's caching, creating a composite action similar to flick's approach

. I'll set up the action file and update the workflow to use blacksmith runners and their cache action. I need to switch to blacksmith runners and extract the setup into a reusable composite action that both jobs can use, mirroring flick's approach. I'll create the composite action file and update the workflow to reference it, making sure to use the same caching strategy flick currently has.

content.text: Rudel already has inline bun caching but runs on `ubuntu-latest`. The flick pattern uses blacksmith runners and extracts setup into a reusable composite action. Let me create that.

name: "Setup Bun"
description: "Setup Bun and install dependencies with caching"
runs:
  using: "composite"
  steps:
    - uses: oven-sh/setup-bun@v2
      with:
        bun-version: 1.3.5

    - uses: actions/cache@v3
      name: Setup bun cache
      with:
        path: ~/.bun/install/cache
        key: ${{ runner.os }}-bun-store-${{ hashFiles('**/bun.lock') }}
        restore-keys: |
          ${{ runner.os }}-bun-store-

    - name: Install dependencies
      run: bun install --frozen-lockfile
      shell: bash

toolUseResult.originalFile:
name: CI

on:
  pull_request:
  push:
    branches:
      - main

jobs:
  verify:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Setup Bun
        uses: oven-sh/setup-bun@v2
        with:
          bun-version: 1.3.5

      - name: Cache bun install
        uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: ~/.bun/install/cache
          key: ${{ runner.os }}-bun-${{ hashFiles('**/bun.lock') }}
          restore-keys: |
            ${{ runner.os }}-bun-

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: bun install --frozen-lockfile

      - name: Verify lockfile unchanged
        run: git diff --exit-code bun.lock

      - name: Verify
        run: bunx turbo run lint check-types test build
        env:
          PG_CONNECTION_STRING: ${{ secrets.PG_CONNECTION_STRING }}
          CLICKHOUSE_URL: ${{ secrets.CLICKHOUSE_URL }}
          CLICKHOUSE_USERNAME: ${{ secrets.CLICKHOUSE_USERNAME }}
          CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD: ${{ secrets.CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD }}
          CLICKHOUSE_DB: ${{ secrets.CLICKHOUSE_DB }}

  deploy:
    if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' && github.event_name == 'push'
    needs: verify
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Setup Fly.io
        uses: superfly/flyctl-actions/setup-flyctl@master

      - name: Deploy
        run: flyctl deploy --remote-only
        env:
          FLY_API_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.FLY_API_TOKEN }}

file.content:
name: CI

on:
  pull_request:
  push:
    branches:
      - main

jobs:
  verify:
    runs-on: blacksmith-2vcpu-ubuntu-2404
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Setup Bun
        uses: ./.github/actions/setup

      - name: Verify lockfile unchanged
        run: git diff --exit-code bun.lock

      - name: Verify
        run: bunx turbo run lint check-types test build
        env:
          PG_CONNECTION_STRING: ${{ secrets.PG_CONNECTION_STRING }}
          CLICKHOUSE_URL: ${{ secrets.CLICKHOUSE_URL }}
          CLICKHOUSE_USERNAME: ${{ secrets.CLICKHOUSE_USERNAME }}
          CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD: ${{ secrets.CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD }}
          CLICKHOUSE_DB: ${{ secrets.CLICKHOUSE_DB }}

  deploy:
    if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' && github.event_name == 'push'
    needs: verify
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Setup Fly.io
        uses: superfly/flyctl-actions/setup-flyctl@master

      - name: Deploy
        run: flyctl deploy --remote-only
        env:
          FLY_API_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.FLY_API_TOKEN }}

content.text:
Done. Here's what changed:

**New file: `.github/actions/setup/action.yml`** — Reusable composite action (mirroring flick's pattern) that:
- Sets up Bun 1.3.5
- Caches `~/.bun/install/cache` keyed on `bun.lock` hash
- Runs `bun install --frozen-lockfile`

**Updated: `.github/workflows/ci.yml`**:
- `verify` job now runs on `blacksmith-2vcpu-ubuntu-2404` (was `ubuntu-latest`)
- Replaced inline bun setup + cache + install steps with `uses: ./.github/actions/setup`
- Deploy job left on `ubuntu-latest` since it only needs flyctl, not bun

content.thinking:
Good, the changes look correct. Now let me commit them. Following the git commit protocol, I should:
1. Run git status to see all changes
2. Stage the files
3. Create a commit with a conventional commit message

The changes are:
1. Created `.github/actions/setup/action.yml` - new composite action
2. Modified `.github/workflows/ci.yml` - updated to use blacksmith runner and new action

This is an improvement/refactoring to CI, so the commit should be `ci:` or `chore:`. Since it's about improving CI caching, `ci:` is appropriate. The message should be something like "ci: use blacksmith runner and bun caching composite action"

@@ -0,0 +1,20 @@
+name: "Setup Bun"
+description: "Setup Bun and install dependencies with caching"
+runs:
+  using: "composite"
+  steps:
+    - uses: oven-sh/setup-bun@v2
+      with:
+        bun-version: 1.3.5
+
+    - uses: actions/cache@v3
+      name: Setup bun cache
+      with:

+        restore-keys: |

+
+    - name: Install dependencies
+      run: bun install --frozen-lockfile
+      shell: bash

content.thinking:
Perfect. Now I'll create the PR with gh pr create. I need to:
- Set title under 80 characters
- Keep description under 5 sentences
- Use --base main

Title: "ci: use blacksmith runner and bun caching composite action"
Description should summarize the changes.

input.command:
gh pr create --base main --title "ci: use blacksmith runner and bun caching composite action" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary
- Extract bun setup, caching, and installation into a reusable composite action at `.github/actions/setup/action.yml`
- Switch CI runner from `ubuntu-latest` to `blacksmith-2vcpu-ubuntu-2404` for faster builds
- Simplify workflow by delegating setup to the composite action

This follows the same pattern as flick to improve CI performance and maintainability.
EOF
)"

content.thinking: Great! The PR has been created successfully. Let me provide the user with the PR URL and a summary.

content.text:
PR created: https://github.com/obsessiondb/rudel/pull/57

**Changes:**
- Created reusable `.github/actions/setup/action.yml` composite action that sets up Bun 1.3.5, caches dependencies, and installs them
- Updated CI workflow to use `blacksmith-2vcpu-ubuntu-2404` runner (faster than `ubuntu-latest`)
- Simplified workflow by delegating setup to the composite action
